item.title: Android 开发的基础知识。 | The Fundamentals of Android Development.
item.description:
德布赖恩208

发表于学习目标

  1. 概述移动应用程序和移动网站之间的区别。
  2. 解释移动操作系统的工作原理。
  3. 安卓操作系统。
  4. 安卓开发简介。

移动平台
移动应用程序是在智能手机设备上运行的可安装软件。 移动应用程序使用设备的硬件和软件功能，通常提供高效、更直观和无缝的用户体验。

移动应用程序和移动网站有什么区别？

移动应用程序是在硬件设备上运行的软件，而移动网站在移动应用程序上运行并且不涉及任何硬件来运行。

移动应用程序和移动网站的优势是什么？

移动应用

  1. 比移动网站更快
  2. 可以访问资源，例如位置、蓝牙等。
  3. 可以在没有互联网访问的情况下工作

移动网站

  1. 建造和维护成本低
  2. 无需从头构建即可与其他平台兼容
  3. 无需 Appstore 批准

注意：要确定是开发移动网站还是移动应用程序，请始终问问自己您希望产品执行哪些具体操作。

移动操作系统如何工作

任何移动设备最基本的软件是其操作系统。 操作系统旨在协调移动设备的硬件和应用程序之间发生的通信。 它管理应用程序的整体体验。

移动操作系统

移动操作系统通常在设备开机时启动，向用户显示不同的应用程序图标和用户界面 (UI) 元素。

操作系统的用途

  1. 允许智能手机、平板电脑和个人数字助理 (PDA) 运行应用程序。
  2. 操作系统提供了一个通道，应用程序可以通过该通道访问处理器、内存、Wi-Fi、蓝牙等设备资源。

最常见的操作系统是Android和iOS 。

安卓

  • 随着 iOS 的普及，开发人员现在拥有更多的控件和对其功能的访问权限。

其他移动操作系统包括运行在 Mozilla Firefox 上的kaiOS 、来自 Jolla 的SailfishOS ，以及 2019 年发布并运行在华为开发的微内核上的华为 HarmonyOS 。 主要用于物联网设备。

安卓平台

  • 作为世界上使用最多的操作系统，Android 不仅限于移动设备。 由于对他人开放，Android 也被用于为其他设备供电。
  • 铬本

    • 能够使用可转换的外形尺寸，不一定要从 ChromeOS 为手机和平板电脑从头开始构建应用程序。

    安卓电视

    • 电视的语音控制功能使您可以完全控制您的设备。

    安卓汽车

    • 它允许您将手机连接到车载显示屏。
    • 它会自动在您的车载显示屏上显示您的应用程序，并使您能够获取行车路线并无缝导航。
    • 磨损操作系统

      • 安卓语言

        • 在 Android 开发领域， Java和Kotlin都是非常流行的编程语言。
        • 比java。

安卓操作系统

  • 近场通信：此功能使电子设备可以轻松进行短距离通信。
  • 自定义主屏幕：Android OS 允许您以您喜欢的方式进一步个性化您的主屏幕。
  • 小部件：用于主屏幕自定义，让您一目了然地查看用户最重要的数据。
  • 应用下载：用户可以通过应用下载释放安卓操作系统的全部潜力。
  • 自定义 ROM ：Android 可以使用自定义和修改版本的 Android 操作系统来访问旧设备上最近的功能。

    • 由 Android 需要运行的组件组成。

  • 应用
  • 应用程序框架
  • 平台库
  • 它们分为 4 层，如下面的架构图所示。

要开始开发应用程序，您需要清楚地了解相关概念。
  • 这些概念包括：

    • 顶级组件

    • 使应用能够通过 BroadcastReceiver、ContentProvider 和 Android SDK 中可访问的 Service 和 Activity 连接到 Internet、拨打电话和拍照。

    活动组件

    • 这些是内容用户在屏幕上与之交互的活动。
    • 单一活动架构模式允许一个活动或相对少量的活动用于整个应用程序。

    安卓视图

    • 这是在屏幕上占据一个矩形区域的设计界面，负责绘图和事件处理。 它显示文本、图像等。

    在 Android 中，每个布局都由一个 XML 文件表示。 这些纯文本文件用作应用程序呈现给用户的界面的蓝图。

    项目文件

    • 配置文件定义项目结构，代码文件提供逻辑，资源文件基本上提供其他一切。

    这基本上是查看创建应用程序时进入的组件。

    • 四个主要组成部分包括：
    1. 活动：这是代表单个用户的用户的入口点。 它允许您将 UI 组件、小部件或用户界面放置在单个屏幕上。 例如，一个音乐播放器应用程序可能有一个活动显示您最喜欢的歌曲列表，另一个活动允许您播放特定歌曲，另一个活动显示热门歌曲列表。
    2. 服务：它们在后台运行，并使用最新更改不断更新数据源和活动。 当用户在应用程序上不活动时，它也会执行任务。 例如，服务的一个很好的例子是一边听音乐一边与某人聊天。
    3. 广播接收器：其目的是实时响应来自其他应用程序或系统的消息。 例如，想象一下，当您收到电池电量不足的通知时，您正在音乐应用程序上欣赏您最喜欢的歌曲。
    4. 内容提供者：负责在应用程序之间共享数据。 例如，一个典型的例子是允许用户在线分享他们的图像的社交媒体应用程序。

    这些 Android 组件通过一个名为AndroidManifest.xml的配置文件耦合在一起。 它用于指定每个组件以及它们如何相互交互。

    可扩展标记语言（XML）

    • 它的主要功能是创建用于对文档、记录、交易和其他几种数据格式的信息进行编码的数据格式。
    • 要使 XML 文档有效，必须满足以下条件：
    1. 文件必须格式正确。
    2. 文档必须符合 XML 语法的所有规则。

    结束。

    感谢您抽出宝贵时间浏览文章！！

    一旦暂停，dbriane208 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

    一旦解除暂停，dbriane208 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

    一旦未发布，dbriane208 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

    如果 dbriane208 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

    感谢您保护 DEV 社区👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 dbriane208 的方法：

    举报其他不当行为

    取消标记 dbriane208 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



    原文:

    Dbriane208

    Posted on Nov 10

    Learning objectives

    1. Provide an overview of the difference between mobile apps and mobile websites.
    2. Explain how the mobile Operating system works.
    3. The Android Operating system.
    4. Introduction to Android development.

    Mobile Platforms
    A mobile app is an installable software that runs on a smartphone device. Mobile apps use the device's hardware, and software features and usually provide an efficient, more intuitive, and seamless user experience.

    What is the difference between a mobile app and a mobile website?

    A mobile app is software that runs on a hardware device whereas a mobile website works on a mobile app and does not involve any hardware to be functional.

    What are the advantages of mobile apps and a mobile website?

    Mobile app

    1. Faster than mobile websites
    2. Can access resources eg location, Bluetooth, etc.
    3. Can work without internet access

    Mobile website

    1. Cheap to build and maintain
    2. Need not to be built from scratch to be compatible with other platforms
    3. No approval is required from Appstore

    Note:To determine whether to develop a mobile website or mobile app always ask yourself what specific actions you expect your product to perform.

    How does a Mobile OS work

    The most fundamental software for any mobile device is its OS. An OS is designed to coordinate communications that occur between the hardware and apps of mobile devices. It manages the overall experiences of an app.

    Mobile operating system

    A mobile OS typically starts up when a device powers on, displaying different application icons and user interface (UI) elements to users.

    Uses of Operating system

    1. Allows smartphones, tablets, and personal digital assistants (PDAs) to run applications.
    2. OS provides a channel with which applications can access device resources such as the processor, memory, Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, and more.

    The most common operating systems are Android and iOS.

    Android

    • Android is a mobile OS that was released in 2008.
    • Android OS is based on a modified version of the Linux kernel and is an open-source software which means anyone can modify it.
    • Android OS was built primarily for smartphones, Chromebook, Android TV, and Android Auto, as well as wearables such as smartwatches.
    • Android has built-in sensors such as gyroscopes and accelerometers, which gives users a multi-touch experience.

    iOS

    • iOS is Apple’s proprietary OS that runs on the iPhone, iPad, and other mobile devices
    • Same as Android, iOS has built-in sensors such as gyroscopes and accelerometers, which gives users a multi-touch experience through several actions such as swiping, pulling, and tapping, and users can seamlessly interact with the screen.
    • With the rise in popularity of iOS, developers now have more controls and access to its features.

    Other mobile operating systems include kaiOS which runs on Mozilla Firefox, SailfishOS from Jolla, and Huawei's HarmonyOS which was released in 2019 and runs on a microkernel that Huawei developed. Mostly used in IoT devices.

    Android Platforms

    • Being the most used operating system in the world, Android is not limited to only mobile devices. Android is also used to power other devices due to its openness to others.
    • Android platforms include, Chromebook, Android TV, Android Auto, and WearOS.

    Chromebook

    • Chromebooks are portable laptops that have support for running Android apps, which gives them more features.
    • Chromebooks operate on Google's ChromeOS.
    • Ability to use convertible form factors to not necessarily build apps from scratch for phones and tablets from ChromeOS.

    Android TV

    • Android TV is designed to bring the mobile experience to your TV.
    • The voice control feature of the TV allows you to have complete control over your devices.

    Android Auto

    • It allows you to connect your phone to your car display.
    • It automatically shows your apps on your car display and makes it possible for you to get driving directions and navigate seamlessly.
    • Android Auto is made to help you stay focused on the road.

    WearOS

    • WearOS is Google's Android OS that is specifically designed to power smartwatches and other wearables.
    • The early release of WearOS allowed watching owners to install WearOS apps through their mobile phones from the google play store.

    Introduction to Android Development

    Android Languages

    • In the world of Android development, Java and Kotlin are both extremely popular programming languages.
    • Java, which was released by Sun Microsystems in 1995 while Kotlin was introduced by JetBrains in 2011.

    -Kotlin has officially become the preferred language for Android app development due to the following reasons; 1. It is concise and standardized hence low code errors. 2. Easier to maintain complex projects due to low code. 3. It is compatible with java hence easy to convert java code to Kotlin. 4. Kotlin handles errors that crash Android apps better

    than java.

    The Android OS

    • Android is a Linux-based OS primarily designed for touchscreen mobile devices such as smartphones and tablets.
    • Some of the unique features and characteristics of the Android OS include:
    1. Near-field communication: This feature makes it easy for electronic devices to communicate over short distances.
    2. Wi-Fi: With this feature, users can connect to various wireless access points around them due to these in-built technologies that support Wi-Fi protocols.
    3. Custom home screen: Android OS allows you to further personalize your home screen the way you like it.
    4. Widgets: Used for home-screen customization to give you a glance view of users' most important data.
    5. App downloads: users can unlock the full potential of the Android operating system through app downloads.
    6. Custom ROMS: Android can use customized and modified versions of Android OS to access features of a recent on an older device.

    The Android OS Architecture

    • Consists of components that Android needs to be functional.
    • Android is built on top of an open-source Linux kernel and other C and C++ libraries exposed via application framework services.

    • The Android OS is a stack of software components roughly divided into five sections:

      1. Applications
      2. Application frameworks
      3. Android runtime
      4. Platform Libraries
      5. Linux Kernel

    • These are separated into 4 layers, as shown in the architecture diagram below.

    Android App Cheatsheet

    • To get started with developing an app, you need a clear understanding of relevant concepts.
    • These concepts include:

    Top-level component

    • Enables apps to connect to the internet, make calls, and take pictures through BroadcastReceiver, ContentProvider, and Service and activity accessible in Android SDK.

    Activity Components

    • These are activities present for content users to interact with on the screen.
    • Single activity architecture pattern allows one activity or a relatively small number of activities to be used for an entire app.

    Android views

    • This is the design interface that occupies a rectangular area on the screen and is responsible for drawing and event handling. It displays text, images, and more.

    Android layout files

    • In Android, each layout is represented by an XML file. These plain text files serve as blueprints for the interface that your application presents to the user.

    Project files

    • Android Project Files belong to one of three main categories: configuration,code, and resource. Configuration files define the project structure, code files provide the logic, and resource files provide essentially everything else.

    Anatomy of An Android App

    • This basically looks into the components that go in when creating an App.

    • An Android app is made up of four major components that serve as the building blocks of any Android app in the market.
    • The four major components include:
    1. Activities: This is the entry point of users that represent a single user. It allows you to place UI components, widgets, or user interfaces on a single screen. eg a music player app may have an activity that shows you a list of your favorite songs, another activity that allows you to play a specific song, and another which shows you a list of trending songs.
    2. Services: They run in the background and constantly update the data sources and activities with the latest changes. It also performs tasks when users are not active on applications. eg A good example of a service is chatting with someone whilst listening to music.
    3. Broadcast Receiver: Its purpose is to respond to messages from other applications or systems in real time. eg,imagine you're enjoying your favorite song on your music app when you get a notification that you're running low on battery power.
    4. Content providers: It's responsible for sharing data between applications. eg A typical example is a social media app that allows users to share their images online.

    These Android components are coupled by a configuration file called AndroidManifest.xml. It is used to specify each component and how they interact with each other.

    Extensible Markup Language(XML)

    • XML (Extensible Markup Language) is used in the presentation of different kinds of data. Its main function is to create data formats that are used to encode information for documentation, records, transactions, and several other data formats.
    • For an XML document to be valid, the following conditions must be fulfilled:
    1. The document must be well-formed.
    2. The document must comply with all the rules of the XML syntax.

    THE END.

    Thank you for taking the time to go through the article!!

    Maame Afia Fordjour - Oct 21

item.title: Javascript 测试 | Javascript Testing
item.description:
丢失按钮

发表于本周我们将有实验室 8和实验室
它还为我们的 SSG 项目引入了一个新工具。 在实验 8中，我们将为 SSG 中的几个功能添加测试功能。 我对此有些经验，因为我的另一门课程CCP555涉及大量测试。 程序中使用的测试框架是 Jest。

程序

观看视频并阅读实验室说明后。 我刚刚选择了我将在本实验 8 中使用的测试框架，即 Jest。 然后我只需使用命令npm install jest将 Jest 安装到我的程序中。 我更新了 package.json 文件中的“test”命令，以使其正确运行。

然后我提取了htmlGen函数并将其放入它自己的文件 htmlGen.js 中，并修改my-button.js以将htmlGen作为外部函数导入。

然后我开始编写测试功能。 起初，我编写的只是 html 良好的价值函数。 所以我为htmlGen()设置了总共 7 个测试：

  • 测试它在哪里生成带有字符串 html 参数的 html 结果
  • 使用错误或空值类型(lang, title, html, string, footer)测试htmlGen()函数

在 CCP555 中，我已经使用npm run coverage了解了覆盖率。 但新的是设置它，以便覆盖范围可以打印出报告。

想法

有几件事我已经知道了，因为我在以前的实验室中的不同课程中做过一些。 虽然对我来说新的是能够打印报道
文件到报告中。 我可能会在不同的项目中更多地使用它。

注意：我没有正确压缩它，因此有几个提交。

医学博士 Khalid Hossen - 一旦暂停，lostbutton 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，lostbutton 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，lostbutton 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 lostbutton 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 您可以执行以下操作来标记 lostbutton：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 lostbutton 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

LostButton

Posted on Nov 10

This week we are going to have Lab 8 and along with the lab
it is also introducing a new tool to our SSG project. In lab 8, we are going to add testing functions for several functions that we have in our SSG. I somewhat have experience with this because one of my other course CCP555 has a lot of testing involved. The testing framework that was used in the program was Jest.

Procedure

After watching the videos and reading through the lab instruction. I just chose the testing framework that I'm going to use in this Lab 8, which is Jest. And then I just install the Jest to my program by using the command npm install jest. I updated the "test" command in the package.json file in order for it to run correctly.

I then extracted the htmlGen function and placed is into its own file that being htmlGen.js, and modified my-button.js to import htmlGen as an external function.

I then started coding the testing functions. At first all I coded was the html good value functions. So I set up a total of 7 tests where for htmlGen():

  • test would generate a html result with a string html argument
  • test where it generates a html result with a string html argument
  • test the htmlGen() function with bad or empty value types (lang, title, html, string, footer)

In CCP555, I have already learned about coverage using npm run coverage. But what was new was setting it up so that coverage would print out a report.

Thought

There were several things I was already aware of since I have done some of this in previous labs in a different course. Though what was new to me was the capability of being able to print coverage
files into a report. I will probably make more use of this on different projects.

NOTE: I didn't squash it correctly therefor having several commits.

Test Function
htmlGen file
Adding Jest
Updatting my-button.js
Coverage Files

Md. Khalid Hossen - Oct 26

item.title: 我在系统的第一个功能中犯的错误 | mistakes I made in my first feature in system
item.description:
这是早期的草稿，已发布以供一些朋友审阅，我将添加更多细节，抱歉英语不好，因为它不是我的母语。

在我目前的工作中，我被要求在系统中设计一个新功能，这是我第一次根据其他人的要求设计一些东西，并且是不依赖于系统中其他组件的全新功能。 当时我很开心！ 我的意思是让我们真正致力于全新的功能和设计，这是一种乐趣。 一开始拿到需求文档的时候，我尽量去了解这个特性背后的业务是什么，在和业务分析人员来回问了很多问题之后，我开始对文档和业务有了更多的了解。

但是在这里我犯了我的第一个错误是

错误一：没有做笔记

当业务分析师向我解释时，我理解它们，但是几分钟过去后，我开始忘记一些细节并一遍又一遍地重复相同的问题，所以几天后我开始在记事本中记笔记不太好，所以现在要做的一件好事是寻找一个好的笔记应用程序。

在理解需求阶段之后，我的下一步是将这个文档分解为任务并在 Jira 中创建它们，在此过程中我犯了几个错误

错误2：忘记前端

那段时间我的全部精力都放在了后端和基础设施上，我脑子里想的是如何让它在后端工作，我做了几个月的工作，并创建了几个任务来实现我的设计目标，当我进入前端时出现错误，原来前端隐藏了一些“后端”的东西，一些小细节可能需要数据库中的额外列，新表。 所以我们可以说我只得到了部分图像而不是完整的图像，这后来导致了代码和表格的一些变化。 老实说，这个错误并不算大，因为后端的代码是可扩展的，并且能够在不做很多更改的情况下添加它们。

解决这个问题的一个好策略可能是将前端设计思维交给队友，这样我们最终可以坐在一起讨论想法以及我们需要什么以及我们理解什么。

错误3：匆忙编码

我在 Jira 中创建任务后直接开始编写代码，这后来产生了很多问题，比如逻辑错误，不符合要求（忘记边缘情况）。 在这里做的一件好事是在编码之前写下我面前每个任务的要求，所以当我到达编码部分时，我可以尝试编写满足这些要求的代码，而不是依赖记忆导致后来忘记一些东西.

即将添加的积分

糟糕的测试，没有再次阅读代码，没有在早期阶段处理一些错误，停止思考设计（这导致错过了一些有助于提高质量和正确性的关键设计点），没有很好地理解早期组件......

一旦暂停，omarkhatib 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被取消。

一旦解除暂停，omarkhatib 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发布，omarkhatib 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 omarkhatib 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 您可以执行以下操作来标记 omarkhatib：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 omarkhatib 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

This is early draft, was published to be reviewed by some friends and I will add more details, sorry for bad English as it's not my native language.

I was asked at my current job to design a new feature in the system, it's first time for me where I design something based on someone else requirements and completely new feature that doesn't rely on other components in the system. I was happy! I mean let's be real working on completely new feature and design it is such a joy. First when I got the requirements document I tried my best to understand what is the business behind the feature, after many questions with back and forth with the business analysist, I start to understand the document and business more.

But here I did my first mistake which is

Mistake 1: No notes were taken

When the business analyst explain stuff to me, I understand them, but when couple of minutes passes, I started to forgot some details and keep repeating same questions over and over again, so after couple of days I start to take notes in notepad which is not so great, so a good thing to do now is to look for a good note taking app.

after the phase of understanding requirements, my next step was to break down this document into tasks and creating them in Jira, In doing that I made a couple of mistakes

Mistake 2 : Forgetting about frontend

My entire focus during that time was on backend and infrastructure all what was in my mind is how I can make it work in the backend, and that what I did for couple of months and I created couple of tasks to reach the goal of my design, the mistake appeared when I reached into the frontend, it turns out that the frontend hides some "backendy" stuff inside of it, some little details that may require extra column in database, new tables. So we can say that I only got part of the image not the full one, which later caused some changes in code and tables. This mistake wasn't huge to be honest, because the code in the backend was extendable and was able to add them without many changes.

Probably a good strategy to solve this problem is to give the frontend design thinking to a teammate so we can sit together in the end and discuss ideas and what we need and what we understand.

Mistake 3 : Rushing into coding

I started to write code directly after creating the tasks in Jira, this created a lot of problems later, Like errors in logic, not meeting the requirements as they are (forgetting edge cases). A good thing to do here was to write down the requirements for each task in front of me before coding, so when I reach coding part I can try to write code that meet those requirements instead of relying on memory that leads later to forgetting some stuff.

Upcoming points to be added

Bad testing, not reading the code again, not dealing with some errors at early stage, stops thinking about design (this led to miss some critical design points that could help in better quality and correctness), not understanding early components very well...
